title: Step labelled 'Type check' actually runs linting, not type checking
reasoning: The step is named 'Type check' but executes `bun run lint`. If the lint script does not include `tsc --noEmit` (or equivalent), actual type errors will silently pass CI while appearing to be checked. Even if lint does include type checking, the misleading name makes it harder to reason about what CI actually validates and can mask a missing dedicated type-check step.
recommendation: Either rename the step to 'Lint' to match what it runs, or add a separate `bun run typecheck` step (the project metadata shows `typecheck: null`, suggesting no typecheck script exists yet). If lint already covers type checking, rename the step to 'Lint & type check'.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included; this is a CI step naming/configuration concern.
minimumFixScope: Rename the step name on line 42 or add a dedicated type-check step.
